title: 5 Botox Mistakes Injectors Make!
excerpt: I'm Dr Sim Pierce and today we're talking about the five botox injection mistakes that I see injectors do often with the best of intentions injectors get these things wrong and end up with a higher risk of side effects and complications that you really don't need to have in the s…

segments.text: I'm Dr Sim Pierce and today we're talking about the five botox injection mistakes that I see injectors do often with the best of intentions injectors get these things wrong and end up with a higher risk of side effects and complications that you really don't need to have in the show I'm going to be going through each mistake and then I'm going to teach you how to avoid them and how to get better results and at the end I've got a special bonus for you that's really going to help you inject more safely and get better results starting tomorrow this might shock you but leaving extra safety margins can actually make things much worse what take for example treating the glabella when injectors first learn to inject botulinum toxin they learned quickly about the risk of eyelid ptosis and we know that the closer you get towards the eyes perhaps the more likelihood there is of causing an eyelid ptosis so what many injectors do is they take the normal boundary and just shift it up another centimeter now when it comes to a muscle like the corrugator if you move a centimeter from where you think that

muscle is you actually move out of that muscle typically into the frontalis muscle and this is what results in many new injectors often causing a central brow drop and a lateral brow lift often known as a spot brow with the best of intentions unfortunately these injectors are causing another side effect instead of just injecting in exactly the right place using an appropriately sized safety margin similarly if you consider the orbicularis oculi muscle injectors who move further away from the eye can often end up more inferior in the cheek than they should be this can cause a saggy appearance as you smile as the cheek is no longer elevated by the lower orbicular circular muscle or even worse if you're deep and inferior you may affect the zygomaticus major causing a droopy smile and this leads me to the next more common mistake which is injecting too deeply it's very important to learn the layers of the face when you're injecting botulinum toxins because you can inject the right Point according to the diagram and the leaflet that you get with your product but end

up injecting underneath the muscle so an example could be what we just mentioned with orbicularisoculi and the zygomaticus major it's very common for new injectors to inject deeply straight through the obicular succuli muscle into the origin of the zygomaticus major this of course is a sure way to make sure your patients aren't smiling after the procedure which is not what we want the next really common area where depth is important is the tail of the corrugator so as you're injecting the corrugator muscle to decrease frown lines if you inject a full depth injection laterally you actually miss the corrugator completely and you end up right near the foramen which could lead to an eyelid ptosis so depth of injection is another common error that new injectors make and I think it's partly down to how injections are taught in mainstream medicine we typically don't vary the layer you put the full needle in and give your injection be it a vaccination or local anesthetic there are various situations that we don't have to be as precise as we do in aesthetic medicine the next common mistake is not recognizing the benefit of movement now

patients often push clinicians in this way and often clinicians simply chase the patient's goal which is Frozen [Music] being frozen does not always mean being more attractive in fact quite often it's the opposite your face ultimately is a tool for communication and collaboration with others and although removing negative Expressions can Aid that process some of the Expressions that you remove are important for building trust one useful example is a flick of the eyebrows apparently it's one of the Key signs that humans look for when you first see someone particularly from a distance is that registration that the person that you know has entered the room it makes people feel there's a small connection there which then you can build on later so zero eyebrow movement does mean you're going to have to use other ways of using your face in order to convey that connection now it is perfectly possible to do this but it's one example of why completely obliterating all movement isn't actually always more attractive because

attractiveness is not about looking like a photograph it's about the ability to connect with people the next common mistake is to overlook the benefits of a muscle that's Contracting in terms of beauty so it's very easy to say that you have lines around your eyes stop the muscle moving and you will get a clearer complexion with fewer distractions and you'll look more beautiful the downside is if you take your orbicular circular as an example it is actually lifting the cheek when you smile so you can end up with a situation where the patient smiles and they have no wrinkles but their cheek doesn't rise up and they therefore look a little bit stunned which is obviously less attractive another interesting example is where the muscle is doing another function so the platysmal bands for example this muscle is an accessory muscle for breathing as evolved in high intensity exercise or in rare instances someone who has COPD for example might use this muscle to Aid deep inspiration and if you completely stop that muscle from moving you could cause a knock-on effective than not actually be able to ventilate quite as effectively this effect is more obvious

in the muscles that we know are functional as well as being communication tools for example orbicularis orisks it causes vertical lip lines and if you treat those without considering the impact on the function of the muscle you end up with a situation where patients can't either drink through a straw or pronunciate certain sounds particularly the sound from any letter any word starting with the letter P and here's another mistake that you really don't want to forget even though it's not common at all to me patients like this but if you meet someone who has almost got an incompetent mouth so they try very hard to close their mouth at rest they're doing this and they have a poppley chin some clinicians end up treating the chin with botulin toxin which then causes incompetence in their ability to close their mouth and you end up with a mouth breather now it's not a good look and makes patients very upset if they don't naturally do this and it happens because we're treating what we see and not considering the function of that muscle always remember the function not just the form we've been through the mistake now let's consider what you can do to

avoid them consider the safety margins first I always say it's a really good thing with a new injector to start to learn about the impact of injections even in places that you don't think you'd ever want to treat this shows real understanding in the face so as you move away from an area that you want to avoid causing a side effect in you're always moving into another area and that other area will have other structures which can absolutely be affected in some way so always brainstorm what is the risk that is increasing as I'm moving away from the risk I'm trying to reduce because there's always another one even if it's just wasting your product that is a risk to your patient they're gonna have to pay for more to get the same effect and if you always ask yourself these questions you will develop a deeper understanding of the face and inject appropriately and realize that everything is a balancing act there is no absolute safe position you're always just bearing the risk and compromising one way or the other we're looking for the best possible compromise with our patients where you get the best result with a minimum set of risks next let's

consider depth so depth obviously starts with understanding the anatomy it's really a good idea to spend some time understanding the layers in the places where you tend to inject we tend to be dealing with just broadly speaking skin a thin layer of fat the muscle that you want to inject and then sometimes fat underneath that and sometimes in the face in particular another muscle underneath that so you need to know which muscle you're aiming for whether it's in the more superficial plane or a deeper plane and which other structures are nearby when you understand this the next step is to start to really understand your needle length and which different positions in the needle correlate to different positions within the face I think it's quite easy just to think about injections as either deep medium or superficial and that covers most of the situations you may face for example if you take the corrugator muscle where we're trying to avoid injecting into the orbits laterally we know that muscle inserts into the surface of the skin so that would be a superficial injection which you would do with the first one third of the needle

if you take the other side of the muscle the Deep part of the corrugator we know that that's a deep muscle near the bone so we know that we want about two-thirds of the needle placed into the skin so this is a great example of how you can know the length of your needle whether it's deep intermediate or superficial and how you can vary that according to where you believe the muscle is that you're aiming for the next problem we need to solve is about appreciating the attractiveness that comes with Movement we don't all need to look like photographs to be attractive there's a lot to be said for the little movements that you can do that Garner someone's connection and attention so the first way to do this is to start appreciating it yourself as an injector movements matter it's also important to bring this story to your patients many patients think the best botox is the most Frozen result and they fixate on being frozen which is understandable because then you look like a photograph you have to essentially sell them the benefits of some movement and explain that your face isn't just about looking like a photograph it's about connecting and forming trust and building

relationships that is what the most beautiful people are able to do most easily is because they are not able to just attract people but also connect with them we're always trying to do both of those things at once and a good clinician will explain that to patients and help them deal with the compromise that we're always making between looking as I say like a photo rather than someone who you can connect with and we need to try and get the balance Just Right Next let's consider how to preserve the function of muscles now part of this is simply about Consulting your patients to accept moderate improvements rather than complete results it doesn't matter which muscle we're treating be it for a gummy smile or ubicularis Oris or the master muscle we always have to have some movement as well and we can't simply freeze to get the aesthetic result what has worked quite well with me is describing the function that they may lose in order to avoid them hoping for the perfect a static result at the expensive function so a big part of this is about communication and communication starts with you understanding that all of these

muscles particularly in the lower face have functional components as well as aesthetic or Community components it makes sense a bit more in other parts of the face that you can treat a little bit more heavily but where there is a function to the muscle we must be very careful and make sure that we're balancing that function with the reduction in a way that still works as an overall healthy picture so I hope you've enjoyed this video it should have helped you understand some of the common mistakes people make when they're trying their best through a lack of a little bit of understanding here and there so now that you understand better how depth and safety margins can go wrong I hope you'll be practicing a safer starting from tomorrow and we've also included that free download view so that you've got a visual reminder of some of what we have discussed if you're interested in this topic you will love two other videos that I've also got on injecting better with botronium toxins we've got one that's especially for the older patient and another one that you can watch is a tutorial just about how to inject botulamp toxin in small foreheads I'm sure you'll find it very
interesting if you haven't seen them already foreign [Music] foreign
